meaning: n.[化]棕土,焦茶色adj.棕色的,棕土的vt.涂红棕色于
examples: One white, the other - burnt umber.
In the midst of all this the lamp still cast a smoky glow, obscure and brown as umber.
The most relative and basic index of service in the library is then umber of books borrowed.
The garden boy, an elf with eyes of burnt umber, had taken to entertaining him with tricks.
The first game was mostly a study in brown and umber (and also red, but you had to add that yourself).
